title: mc-webui: Web UI wrapper for meshcore-cli

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Hi I was invited here from the meshcore-cli repo. I’m the author of mc-webui, a small web UI wrapper around meshcore-cli. Repo: https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui Happy to hear any feedback or ideas
messages.content_html: Hi I was invited here from the meshcore-cli repo. I’m the author of mc-webui, a small web UI wrapper around meshcore-cli. Repo: https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui Happy to hear any feedback or ideas
embeds_html:
GitHub - MarekWo/mc-webui: A lightweight web interface for meshcore...
A lightweight web interface for meshcore-cli, providing browser-based access to MeshCore mesh network. - MarekWo/mc-webui
messages.reactions_html: 5

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: very cool! keep us posted with your progress!
messages.content_html: very cool! keep us posted with your progress!
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: ka.RFz
messages.text: Thanks for sharing, this is awesome!
messages.content_html: Thanks for sharing, this is awesome!
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: LittleTiTi
messages.text: Hello, I have a major problem: no received messages are displayed on the webpage. Sending messages works fine, as does accessing the channel or contact list and sending advertisements. There are no errors in the logs; I've tried two different Raspberry Pis and two different modules. I do get the notification with the bell icon for new messages, but nothing appears on the screen once I select a channel, and the same happens with direct messages. Thank you in advance for your help.
messages.content_html: Hello, I have a major problem: no received messages are displayed on the webpage. Sending messages works fine, as does accessing the channel or contact list and sending advertisements. There are no errors in the logs; I've tried two different Raspberry Pis and two different modules. I do get the notification with the bell icon for new messages, but nothing appears on the screen once I select a channel, and the same happens with direct messages. Thank you in advance for your help.

messages.author: ka.RFz
messages.text: I just had some time to try this out, yeah same issue here. Outbound messages work but nothing shows up as received.
messages.content_html: I just had some time to try this out, yeah same issue here. Outbound messages work but nothing shows up as received.

messages.author: LittleTiTi
messages.text: You must install dev branch, the problem is solve
messages.content_html: You must install dev branch, the problem is solve
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: ka.RFz
messages.text: Thanks, will give it a try
messages.content_html: Thanks, will give it a try

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: In fact I recently merged main with dev, so currently they are on the same version
messages.content_html: In fact I recently merged main with dev, so currently they are on the same version

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Today I added some nice functionality to the app - pictures are now displayed in the chat based on a URL
messages.content_html: Today I added some nice functionality to the app - pictures are now displayed in the chat based on a URL

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: You can also manage your rpt directly from the app
messages.content_html: You can also manage your rpt directly from the app
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: New Contact Management panel
messages.content_html: New Contact Management panel

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: And Existing Contacts panel for viewing
messages.content_html: And Existing Contacts panel for viewing

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: New feature -- filtering pending contacts (before adding them to the device).
messages.content_html: New feature -- filtering pending contacts (before adding them to the device).

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: New feature - unread DM messages and pending contacts badges are now displayed on the floating buttons.
messages.content_html: New feature - unread DM messages and pending contacts badges are now displayed on the floating buttons.
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: I totally rewrote the Installation guide. It now makes much more sense for those who install it for the first time. I went through it step by step to make sure nothing is missing. Let me know what you think! https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui?tab=readme-ov-file#installation
messages.content_html: I totally rewrote the Installation guide. It now makes much more sense for those who install it for the first time. I went through it step by step to make sure nothing is missing. Let me know what you think! https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui?tab=readme-ov-file#installation

GitHub - MarekWo/mc-webui: A lightweight web interface for meshcore...
A lightweight web interface for meshcore-cli, providing browser-based access to MeshCore mesh network. - MarekWo/mc-webui

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: README doc was RADICALLY refactored and simplified to make it way easier to read. I highly recommend checking this out now. https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui
messages.content_html: README doc was RADICALLY refactored and simplified to make it way easier to read. I highly recommend checking this out now. https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: incrediblediy
messages.text: has anyone run this on bare metal Windows without docker ? like a python script or so
messages.content_html: has anyone run this on bare metal Windows without docker ? like a python script or so

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: This project was dockerized to make it extremely simple to install, so Windows Docker Desktop is required to make it work. But of course the underlying meshore-cli package should work fine on Windows without Docker.
messages.content_html: This project was dockerized to make it extremely simple to install, so Windows Docker Desktop is required to make it work. But of course the underlying meshore-cli package should work fine on Windows without Docker.

messages.author: Craig VK2BTQ
messages.text: I notice in the instructions it only talks about a USB companion. Is it possible to set it up to WiFi companion?
messages.content_html: I notice in the instructions it only talks about a USB companion. Is it possible to set it up to WiFi companion?

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: I did not test it that. The point is that the Linux server needs to communicate with the device, but I would need to try that on my end. I will let you know once I find out.
messages.content_html: I did not test it that. The point is that the Linux server needs to communicate with the device, but I would need to try that on my end. I will let you know once I find out.
messages.reply_html:
Craig VK2BTQ
I notice in the instructions it only talks about a USB companion. Is it possible to set it up to WiFi companion?

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: BREAKING NEWS! I added an experimental Meshcli Console to the application which allows to communicate with the device with meshcore-cli commands. Some examples on the screenshots
messages.content_html: BREAKING NEWS! I added an experimental Meshcli Console to the application which allows to communicate with the device with meshcore-cli commands. Some examples on the screenshots
messages.reactions_html: 3

messages.author: Craig VK2BTQ
messages.text: No worries. It's just that I don't know which server my container will be on so easier if it just talks over the network.
messages.content_html: No worries. It's just that I don't know which server my container will be on so easier if it just talks over the network.
messages.reply_html:
MarWoj
I did not test it that. The point is that the Linux server needs to communicate with the device, but I would need to try that on my end. I will let you know once I find out.

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: The application installation process was tremendously simplified. Now you practically do not need to do any configuration before the first run. Both device name and the serial port name are now automatically detected which should work right away in 99% of cases. Please check it out if you have not done so yet https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui
messages.content_html: The application installation process was tremendously simplified. Now you practically do not need to do any configuration before the first run. Both device name and the serial port name are now automatically detected which should work right away in 99% of cases. Please check it out if you have not done so yet https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: New handy feature was added - the node map, both general and individual
messages.content_html: New handy feature was added - the node map, both general and individual
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Quotation was added to the chat window
messages.content_html: Quotation was added to the chat window
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Some handy updates: 1. Improved display of the chat window - colored avatars 2. You can now check if there are any app updates available
messages.content_html: Some handy updates: 1. Improved display of the chat window - colored avatars 2. You can now check if there are any app updates available

messages.author: FAte_CzE
messages.text: thanks!
messages.content_html: thanks!
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: I added a remote app upgrade utility. You can now up upgrade your app without remoting to the server via SSH. For details-- check the Updating chapter of the README doc. https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui (edited)
messages.content_html: I added a remote app upgrade utility. You can now up upgrade your app without remoting to the server via SSH. For details-- check the Updating chapter of the README doc. https://github.com/MarekWo/mc-webui (edited)

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Auto-cleanup was added. You can setup cleanup criteria and cleanup time (hour). Contacts marked as Protected will not be automatically deleted. (edited)
messages.content_html: Auto-cleanup was added. You can setup cleanup criteria and cleanup time (hour). Contacts marked as Protected will not be automatically deleted. (edited)

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Contacts can be protected from deletion in the Contact Management section
messages.content_html: Contacts can be protected from deletion in the Contact Management section

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Now, @mentions work in the app!
messages.content_html: Now, @mentions work in the app!
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Console command memory was added. Also, slow command timout was handled so now commands like node_discover does not timeout prematurely.
messages.content_html: Console command memory was added. Also, slow command timout was handled so now commands like node_discover does not timeout prematurely.

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: and... Heard by X repeater(s) info is not added to sent messages. Just like in the Meshcore app.
messages.content_html: and... Heard by X repeater(s) info is not added to sent messages. Just like in the Meshcore app.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: Live demo is now available. PLEASE use with caution. https://mc-webui.c11.net.pl/
messages.content_html: Live demo is now available. PLEASE use with caution. https://mc-webui.c11.net.pl/
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: You can now check your message status in MeshCore Analyzer--right from the chat window!
messages.content_html: You can now check your message status in MeshCore Analyzer--right from the chat window!

embeds_html:
mc-webui v2 — Major Release Announcement · MarekWo mc-webui ...
The wait is over. After months of development and testing, mc-webui v2 is merging into the main branch this Saturday (March 29, 2026). This is the biggest update since the project began — a complet...
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MarWoj
messages.text: The v2 update was successful-- it has been merged into the main branch. The application was thoroughly redesigned and reengineered. The meshcore-cli engine was replaced with the native backend which made it run faster and become by far more reliable than ever before. I highly recommend checking it out!
messages.content_html: The v2 update was successful-- it has been merged into the main branch. The application was thoroughly redesigned and reengineered. The meshcore-cli engine was replaced with the native backend which made it run faster and become by far more reliable than ever before. I highly recommend checking it out!
messages.reactions_html: 1

embeds_html:
Contact Management in mc-webui
A deep dive into contact management in mc-webui: device contacts, the cache layer, ignored and blocked flags, recommended settings, and migration from the official MeshCore apps.
